title: Dried Edible Mushrooms Grading and Marking Rules 1972
description: Dried Edible Mushrooms Grading and Marking Rules 1972
text: DRIED EDIBLE MUSHROOMS GRADING AND MARKING RULES 1. Short title and application—(1) These rules may be called Dried Edible Mushrooms Grading and Marking Rules, 1972. (2) They shall be apply to dried edible mushrooms of the varieties produced in lndia. 2.‘ Definitions.—ln these rules:— (1) "Agricultural Marketing Adviser" means the; Agricultural Marketing Adviser to the Government of India; _(2) 'lMushroom" means dried edible mushroom; , (3) j“Schedule" means a Schedule appended to these rules. ' 3. Grade deskignation.— The grade designations to indicate the quality of dried edible mushrooms shall he as set out in column 2 'of Schedule I. 4. Definition of quality.-The quality indicated, by the respective grade designations shall be as set out against each grade designation in columns 3 to 6 and the general characteristics applicable to all the grades asset out in column 7 of Schedules l. 5. Grade designation marlin—The grade designation mark shall consist’of a label specifying the grade designation and bearing a design} (consisting of an outline map of India with the word “AGMARK” and the figures of the rising sun, with the words "Produce of India" and ("W arm" ) resembling the one as set out in Schedule II. 6. Method of marking—(1) The grade designation mark label shall be securely affixed to each container in a manner approved by the Agricultural Marketing Adviser and shall clearly show the following particulars narnely:—-. (a) Commodity and grade designation. (b) Net weight. (c) Date and place of packing. (2) An authorised packer may, after 1‘ obtaining the prior approval of the Agricultural Marketing Adviser, mark his private trade mark on a container, in a mannerrapproved by the said Officer or an Officerjauthorised by him in this behalf, provided that the private trade mark does not 6—264 A.G. & Oo—OD./2005 ,33 represent - quality or grade of edible mushrooms different from that indicated by 7 the grade designation mark affixed to the container in accordance with these rules. 7. Method of packing—4(1) Only strong, sound, clean and dry and durable containers, as may be prescribed by the Agricultural Marketing Advisor shall be used for packing. They shall be the horn any insect or fungus infestation and shall also be free from any undesirable odour. (2) The containers shall be securely closed and sealed in such manner as may be prescribed by the Agricultural Marketing Advisor. (3) Each package shall contain only dried edible mushroom of the varieties mentioned in Schedule I. 8. Special conditions of certificate of , Authorisation.——In addition to the conditions specified in rule 4 of the General Grading and Marking Rules, 1937, the following special conditions shall be the observed by packers to the satisfaction of the Agricultural Marketing Adviser namely:—‘ (a) An authorised packer shall make such ' arrangements for dried edible mushrooms as may be prescribed by the Agricultural Marketing Adviser, (b) An authorised packer shall provide all facilities to the Inspecting Officer duly authorised by the Agricultural Marketing Adviser for sampling, testing and such other matters as may be necessary in connection with grading and Agmarking of dried edible mushrooms. 9. Fumigation.—If an Agmarked dried edible mushroom consignment is exported by land or water route, then it shall be necessary to get the consignment fumigated at exporters‘ cost within 15 days of actual export through the authorised fumigation agencies duly supported by a Fumigation Certificate issued by , the said agency. For consignment of dried edible mushroom sent by air, fumigation may be voluntary. -- 1 of 2 -- Grade'designations and driflnition of quality of dried edible mushrooms (Gnu-h) (black Indian morels) of the botanical varieties Morehall esculenta, SCHEDULE 1 (See Rules 3 and 4) Morchcll auqusticeps and Morehella coniu. The produce shall have characteristic flavour of dried ‘ edible mushroom and shall be free from otfdlavou J and the produce shall be of the current haves! year unless specifically mentioned otherwise Tyre Air) “use: Dried edible rrnlshroom having a maximum tail length prescribed for the next lower grade shall be allowed upto a maximum of5 per cent by weight (a) Tail.- Means stalk of dried edible mushroom above the sand-bags and sandpocket above the root. 11.5. Grade — Mains the produce which does not conform to any of the gades l to .5 in this schedule but is in accordance with the specifications of importer (buyer) contained iu‘afinn order (Firm Order means an order given by an importer abroad to an exporter in India which isaccompaniod by a letter» of Credit or to her documentary evidence to show that the exporta’ has received an order or a grade of Credit edible mushroom different in specifications to r the Agrrnrk grades ) Brokens.- Means heads which have lost more than one-fourths of the surface but are larger than the pieces (Head means the honey—combed conical ark umbrella shaped structure above the talk or the tail of edible mushroom). ,‘ Pieces.— Means pieces of head ofany of the above three varieties of mushrooms measure less than 0.5 cm. n length. Foreign nutter.- Means sand, sand-bags, stones, splinters, mud and any other organic or inorganic foreign matter Sand hogs pockets. — Means the bottom portion of the stalk usually filled with earth or sand. . Harvest year — Means the year beginning horn lst April and ending on Si“ March coinciding with the Export year beginning from Ist April and ending or 3 1“ March each year. » : SCHEDULE II (See Rule 5) Design for the grade designation mark {picture as depicted on page 14 (Schedule- 1)} 34' -- 2 of 2 --
